title: evaluate() Vec<SieveAction> always has exactly one element
description: evaluate() returns Vec<SieveAction> but always returns a Vec with exactly one element. Callers who read the signature and docs expect multi-action scripts (e.g. fileinto + keep, implicit-keep fallback, multiple fileinto). The current implementation collapses to a single action and returns a singleton Vec. This generates repeated 'why can I only get one action?' issues. API should either return Option<SieveAction>/SieveAction directly if single-action semantics are intentional, or actually support multiple actions, or document the invariant prominently.
